page.content: The analysis of calcified cartilage reveals significant findings in Table 1, with the header categories being Acetabular labrum, Femoral head, and Medial meniscus, each subdivided into Number and Percentage. The row labeled Total calcified cartilage (CC) shows values of 85/85 and 100% for Acetabular labrum, 82/85 and 96.5% for Femoral head, and 84/85 and 98.8% for Medial meniscus. In the Bilateral CC row, the values are 85/85 and 100% for Acetabular labrum, 68/85 and 80.0% for Femoral head, and 79/85 and 92.9% for Medial meniscus.

page.content: The Unilateral CC row displays 0/85 and 0% for Acetabular labrum, 14/85 and 16.5% for Femoral head, and 5/85 and 5.9% for Medial meniscus. The Left CC row reports 85/85 and 100% for Acetabular labrum, 75/85 and 88.2% for Femoral head, and 80/85 and 94.1% for Medial meniscus. Finally, the Right CC row shows 85/85 and 100% for Acetabular labrum, 75/85 and 88.2% for Femoral head, and 83/85 and 97.7% for Medial meniscus. Table 2 presents demographic data with headers Name, Age, No. of Surviving Children, No. of Failed Pregnancies, Total no. of marriages, Total no. of spousal marriages, and Endogamous? (Y/N). The individuals listed include Hashim (38, 1, 0, 1, 1, Y), Nasir (39, 0, 0, 2, 2, Unknown), Zafar (24, 0, 1, 1, 1, Y), Fazal Din (42, 1, 0, 1, 1, y), Sadaqat (36, 0, 0, 1, 1, Y), Asif (41, 2, 0, 1, 1, N), Ijaz (34, 0, 2, 2, 1, Y, N), and Ali (44, 0, 0, 3, 2, N).
